Details: Bulgarian split squat / RDL / step-ups / core
WorkoutDescription:
Ultra Goal: Establish single-leg strength foundation to maintain running form late in long efforts.

WARM-UP (6 min): Leg swings x15 each, Hip circles x10 each, Glute bridges x15, Bodyweight squats x10

MAIN BLOCK:
A1. Bulgarian Split Squat — 3x10 each leg — Rest 60s
A2. Romanian Deadlift — 3x10 — Rest 90s
B1. Single-leg Step-ups (box) — 3x10 each leg — Rest 60s
B2. Glute Bridge — 3x12 — Rest 60s
C1. Single-leg Calf Raise (full ROM) — 3x12 each — Rest 45s
C2. Nordic Hamstring Curl (eccentric only) — 3x5 — Rest 60s

CORE CIRCUIT (x3, 45s rest between rounds):
Plank 45s | Dead Bugs 12 reps | Pallof Press 10 each side

COOL-DOWN (5 min): Hip flexor stretch 60s each, Pigeon pose 60s each, Ankle circles 30s each

Details: Flat or rolling terrain, finish with 4x20s strides
WorkoutDescription: EASY RUN: 45min at conversational pace (130-145 bpm). Should feel easy - slow down if HR drifts above 145. STRIDES: 4x20s gradual acceleration to 90% max speed (Z5 ~181+), walk back recovery. Focus on smooth form, not all-out sprinting.

TEMPO SESSION: 10min warm-up building from Z1 to Z2. Main set: 3x10min at tempo pace (HR 150-165, feels comfortably hard, can speak short sentences only). 2min easy jog between intervals. 10min cool-down in Z1. Tempo should feel sustainable but challenging.

Details: Push-ups / rows / shoulder press / core / mobility
WorkoutDescription:
Ultra Goal: Build upper body endurance for pole usage and maintain posture on long climbs.

WARM-UP (5 min): Arm circles x15 each way, Cat-cow x10, Thoracic rotations x10 each

MAIN BLOCK:
A1. Push-ups — 3x10 — Rest 60s
A2. Bent-over Rows (DB or band) — 3x10 — Rest 60s
B1. Shoulder Press (light) — 3x12 — Rest 60s
B2. Single-leg Glute Bridge — 3x10 each — Rest 45s
C1. Single-leg Calf Raise — 2x15 each — Rest 45s

CORE CIRCUIT (x3, 45s rest between rounds):
Russian Twists 15 each side | Side Plank 30s each | Dead Bugs 12 reps

MOBILITY (10 min): Hip flexor stretch 90s each, 90/90 stretch 60s each, Thoracic spine foam roll 2 min, Ankle dorsiflexion stretch 60s each

Details: Optional light jog or full rest
WorkoutDescription: REST DAY: Full rest preferred. If legs feel good, optional 20-30min very easy jog keeping HR under 130. This should feel like you could do it forever. If in doubt, rest.

LONG RUN: 2 hours at easy aerobic pace. HR should stay 130-145 throughout. Walk uphills if HR pushes above 145. Practice fueling: take 30-40g carbs every 45min (gel, bar, or drink). Stay hydrated. Focus on time on feet, not pace or distance.

RECOVERY SESSION: 45min very easy movement. Can be easy jog (HR under 140) or bike spin (low resistance). Goal is blood flow for recovery, not fitness. Should feel effortless. If legs are tired from Saturday, do bike instead of run.

Details: Bulgarian split squat / RDL / lunges / core
WorkoutDescription:
Ultra Goal: Progress single-leg strength to improve running economy and reduce ground contact time.

WARM-UP (6 min): Leg swings x15 each, Hip circles x10 each, Bodyweight walking lunges x10, Calf raises x15

MAIN BLOCK:
A1. Bulgarian Split Squat — 3x10 each leg (+2.5kg from W1) — Rest 60s
A2. Romanian Deadlift — 3x10 (+2.5kg from W1) — Rest 90s
B1. Walking Lunges — 3x10 each leg — Rest 60s
B2. Hip Thrust — 3x12 — Rest 60s
C1. Single-leg Calf Raise (full ROM) — 3x12 each — Rest 45s
C2. Nordic Hamstring Curl (eccentric) — 3x5 — Rest 60s

CORE CIRCUIT (x3, 45s rest between rounds):
Plank 50s | Dead Bugs 14 reps | Pallof Press 10 each side

COOL-DOWN (5 min): Hip flexor stretch 60s each, Pigeon pose 60s each, Hamstring stretch 60s each
EASY RUN: 50min at conversational pace. HR 130-145. Nose breathing should be possible most of the time. If you can't hold a conversation, you're going too fast. This builds your aerobic engine.

HILL REPEATS: 10min easy warm-up. Find a steep hill (8-12% grade). 8x2min hard uphill effort - HR will climb to 165-180+ by end of each rep. Push into Z5 on final 30s if feeling good. Jog/walk down for recovery (HR should drop below 140 before next rep). 10min easy cool-down. Focus: powerful glute drive, quick cadence, forward lean.

Details: Push-ups / rows / shoulder press / core / mobility
WorkoutDescription:
Ultra Goal: Maintain upper body and core endurance; prioritize hip mobility for efficient uphill mechanics.

WARM-UP (5 min): Arm circles x15 each, Cat-cow x10, Thoracic rotations x10 each

MAIN BLOCK:
A1. Push-ups — 3x12 — Rest 60s
A2. Bent-over Rows — 3x12 — Rest 60s
B1. Shoulder Press (light) — 3x12 — Rest 60s
B2. Single-leg Glute Bridge — 3x12 each — Rest 45s
C1. Single-leg Calf Raise — 2x15 each — Rest 45s

CORE CIRCUIT (x3, 45s rest between rounds):
Russian Twists 15 each side | Side Plank 35s each | Dead Bugs 14 reps

MOBILITY (10 min): Hip flexor stretch 90s each, 90/90 stretch 60s each, Thoracic spine extension 2 min, Ankle dorsiflexion stretch 60s each

Details: Optional light jog or full rest
WorkoutDescription: REST DAY: Prioritize rest. If very fresh, optional 20-30min Z1 jog.

LONG RUN: 2h15 on trail with elevation. Keep HR in Z2 (130-145) - this means walking uphills. Practice hiking technique on steep sections: short steps, hands on thighs if needed. Fuel every 45min. Build duration gradually.
RECOVERY: 45min easy. Bike preferred if legs are fatigued from Saturday's hills.

Details: Split squat / RDL / step-downs / Nordic curls / core
WorkoutDescription:
Ultra Goal: Introduce step-downs for downhill resilience; continue progressive overload on single-leg patterns.

WARM-UP (6 min): Leg swings x15 each, Hip circles x10 each, Glute bridges x15, Bodyweight squats x10

MAIN BLOCK:
A1. Bulgarian Split Squat — 3x10 each leg (+2.5kg from W2) — Rest 60s
A2. Romanian Deadlift — 3x10 (+2.5kg from W2) — Rest 90s
B1. Step-downs (slow, controlled) — 3x8 each leg — Rest 60s
B2. Hip Thrust — 3x12 — Rest 60s
C1. Single-leg Calf Raise (full ROM) — 3x15 each — Rest 45s
C2. Nordic Hamstring Curl (eccentric) — 3x6 — Rest 60s

CORE CIRCUIT (x3, 45s rest between rounds):
Plank 55s | Dead Bugs 15 reps | Side Plank 35s each

COOL-DOWN (5 min): Hip flexor stretch 60s each, Pigeon pose 60s each, Hamstring stretch 60s each

Details: Flat or rolling, finish with 5x20s strides
WorkoutDescription: EASY RUN + STRIDES: 50min easy (Z2), then 5x20s strides accelerating to near-max speed (Z5). Full walk recovery between strides. Strides improve running economy and provide brief Z5 stimulus without fatigue cost.

TEMPO SESSION: Longer intervals this week. 10min warm-up. 2x20min at tempo (HR 150-165). 3min easy jog between. 10min cool-down. Mental challenge: stay locked in for full 20min. Pace should feel hard but sustainable.

Details: Push-ups / rows / core / hip & ankle mobility
WorkoutDescription:
Ultra Goal: Develop anti-rotation core strength and thoracic mobility for efficient uphill posture.

WARM-UP (5 min): Arm circles x15 each, Cat-cow x10, World's greatest stretch x5 each

MAIN BLOCK:
A1. Push-ups — 3x12 — Rest 60s
A2. Bent-over Rows — 3x12 — Rest 60s
B1. Shoulder Press — 3x10 — Rest 60s
B2. Single-leg Glute Bridge — 3x12 each — Rest 45s
C1. Single-leg Calf Raise — 3x15 each — Rest 45s

CORE CIRCUIT (x3, 45s rest between rounds):
Pallof Press 12 each side | Plank 50s | Russian Twists 15 each side

MOBILITY (10 min): Hip flexor stretch 90s each, 90/90 stretch 60s each, Ankle dorsiflexion mobilization 60s each, Thoracic rotations 60s each

Details: Optional light jog or full rest
WorkoutDescription: REST DAY: Rest preferred before big weekend.
LONG RUN: 2h30 - longest run yet. Stay in Z2. Practice race nutrition: aim for 60g carbs/hour (gel every 30min or equivalent). Note what works and what doesn't. Hydration: 500ml/hour minimum.
RECOVERY: 50min easy. Essential after 2h30 long run. Keep it truly easy.

Ultra Goal: Active recovery — maintain movement patterns at reduced load to absorb 3 weeks of training.

WARM-UP (5 min): Leg swings x10 each, Hip circles x10 each, Bodyweight squats x10

MAIN BLOCK (light):
A1. Bodyweight Bulgarian Split Squat — 2x8 each leg — Rest 45s
A2. Romanian Deadlift (50% weight) — 2x8 — Rest 60s
B1. Bodyweight Walking Lunges — 2x8 each leg — Rest 45s
B2. Glute Bridge — 2x12 — Rest 45s
C1. Calf Raise (bodyweight) — 2x15 — Rest 30s

CORE (1 round):
Plank 45s | Dead Bugs 10 reps | Side Plank 30s each

MOBILITY (15 min): Hip flexor stretch 90s each, Pigeon pose 90s each, 90/90 stretch 60s each, Hamstring stretch 60s each, Ankle dorsiflexion 60s each, Thoracic foam roll 2 min
DELOAD RUN: 40min very easy. HR should sit at lower end of Z2 (130-138). Enjoy the easy effort. Body is adapting to previous 3 weeks.
EASY RUN: Another easy day. No tempo, no intervals. Just easy running. This is when fitness actually builds - during recovery.
FULL REST: No exercise. Sleep well, eat well, hydrate.
REST OR EASY: Light 20min jog if feeling restless, otherwise rest.
DELOAD LONG RUN: Only 1h30 this week. Keep it flat and easy. No fueling practice needed for this duration.
RECOVERY: Easy bike or walk. Prepare for next training block.

Details: Split squat / RDL / step-ups / Nordic curls / core
WorkoutDescription:
Ultra Goal: Reset to full load after deload; build toward peak single-leg strength for mountain terrain.

WARM-UP (6 min): Leg swings x15 each, Hip circles x10 each, Glute bridges x15, A-skips x20

MAIN BLOCK:
A1. Bulgarian Split Squat — 4x8 each leg — Rest 75s
A2. Romanian Deadlift — 4x8 — Rest 90s
B1. Single-leg Step-ups (higher box) — 3x10 each leg — Rest 60s
B2. Hip Thrust — 3x12 — Rest 60s
C1. Single-leg Calf Raise (full ROM) — 3x15 each — Rest 45s
C2. Nordic Hamstring Curl — 3x6 — Rest 60s

CORE CIRCUIT (x3, 45s rest between rounds):
Plank 60s | Dead Bugs 15 reps | Pallof Press 12 each side

COOL-DOWN (5 min): Hip flexor stretch 60s each, Pigeon pose 60s each

Details: Flat or rolling, 5x20s strides at end
WorkoutDescription: EASY RUN + STRIDES: 50min Z2 running + 5x20s strides (Z5). Coming off deload week - should feel fresh.

HILL SESSION WITH Z5 SPRINTS: 10min warm-up. 10x2min hill repeats (Z4). Then 4x10s MAXIMAL hill sprints (Z5+, all-out effort, very short). Full 2min recovery between sprints. 10min cool-down. Hill sprints build explosive power and neuromuscular recruitment.

Ultra Goal: Strengthen upper body and core endurance for maintaining form during fatigued late-race miles.

WARM-UP (5 min): Arm circles x15 each, Cat-cow x10, Thoracic rotations x10 each

MAIN BLOCK:
A1. Push-ups — 3x12 — Rest 60s
A2. Bent-over Rows — 3x12 — Rest 60s
B1. Shoulder Press — 3x10 — Rest 60s
B2. Single-leg Glute Bridge — 3x12 each — Rest 45s
C1. Single-leg Calf Raise — 3x12 each — Rest 45s

CORE CIRCUIT (x3, 45s rest between rounds):
Russian Twists 15 each | Side Plank 40s each | Dead Bugs 15 reps

MOBILITY (10 min): Hip flexor stretch 90s each, 90/90 stretch 60s each, Thoracic extension 2 min, Ankle mobilization 60s each

Details: Optional light jog or full rest
WorkoutDescription: REST: Prioritize rest before big weekend.

Details: 1000m+ D+ target - walk uphills to stay in zone
WorkoutDescription: LONG RUN WITH VERTICAL: 2h45 targeting 1000m+ elevation gain. This means finding mountains or repeating a hilly loop. Walk ALL uphills to keep HR in Z2. Practice hiking poles if you'll use them in races. Fuel every 40-45min.
RECOVERY: Essential easy session after big vertical day. Bike may be better for leg recovery.

Details: Split squat / RDL / lunges / step-downs / core
WorkoutDescription:
Ultra Goal: Add step-down volume to build eccentric quad endurance for sustained downhill sections.

WARM-UP (6 min): Leg swings x15 each, Hip circles x10 each, Bodyweight lunges x10, Calf raises x15

MAIN BLOCK:
A1. Bulgarian Split Squat — 4x8 each leg (+2.5kg from W5) — Rest 75s
A2. Romanian Deadlift — 4x8 (+2.5kg from W5) — Rest 90s
B1. Step-downs (controlled 2s lowering) — 3x10 each leg — Rest 60s
B2. Walking Lunges — 3x10 each leg — Rest 60s
C1. Single-leg Calf Raise (full ROM) — 3x15 each — Rest 45s
C2. Nordic Hamstring Curl — 3x6 — Rest 60s

CORE CIRCUIT (x3, 45s rest between rounds):
Plank 60s | Pallof Press 12 each | Side Plank 35s each

COOL-DOWN (5 min): Hip flexor stretch 60s each, Hamstring stretch 60s each, Pigeon pose 60s each
EASY RUN: 55min at conversational pace. Focus on relaxed form.
TEMPO SESSION: 3x15min tempo intervals. Total 45min at threshold. Challenging session - stay mentally focused. HR 150-165.

Details: Push-ups / rows / core / ankle & hip mobility
WorkoutDescription:
Ultra Goal: Maintain upper body endurance and prioritize ankle/hip mobility for technical trail stability.

WARM-UP (5 min): Arm circles x15 each, Cat-cow x10, World's greatest stretch x5 each

MAIN BLOCK:
A1. Push-ups — 3x15 — Rest 60s
A2. Bent-over Rows — 3x12 — Rest 60s
B1. Shoulder Press — 3x10 — Rest 60s
B2. Single-leg Glute Bridge — 3x12 each — Rest 45s
C1. Single-leg Calf Raise — 3x12 each — Rest 45s

CORE CIRCUIT (x3, 45s rest between rounds):
Pallof Press 12 each | Plank 55s | Dead Bugs 15 reps

MOBILITY (10 min): Hip flexor stretch 90s each, Ankle dorsiflexion stretch 60s each, 90/90 stretch 60s each, Thoracic foam roll 2 min

Details: Optional light jog or full rest
WorkoutDescription: REST: Full rest recommended before 3h long run.

LONG RUN - 3 HOUR MILESTONE: First 3h run. This is race-relevant duration. Stay disciplined in Z2. Practice your exact race nutrition - aim for 60-80g carbs/hour. Note energy levels, GI comfort, hydration needs. Walk breaks are fine.
RECOVERY: Very important after 3h effort. Keep it truly easy.

Details: Split squat / RDL / eccentric step-downs / Nordic curls / core
WorkoutDescription:
Ultra Goal: Introduce eccentric tempo work to prepare quads and hamstrings for sustained mountain descents.

WARM-UP (6 min): Leg swings x15 each, Hip circles x10 each, Glute bridges x15, A-skips x20

MAIN BLOCK:
A1. Bulgarian Split Squat — 4x8 each leg @3010 tempo (3s down) — Rest 75s
A2. Romanian Deadlift — 4x8 — Rest 90s
B1. Step-downs (slow 3s lowering) — 3x10 each leg — Rest 60s
B2. Hip Thrust — 3x12 — Rest 60s
C1. Single-leg Calf Raise (full ROM, slow eccentric) — 3x15 each — Rest 45s
C2. Nordic Hamstring Curl — 3x6 — Rest 60s

CORE CIRCUIT (x3, 45s rest between rounds):
Plank 60s | Dead Bugs 15 reps | Pallof Press 12 each

COOL-DOWN (5 min): Hip flexor stretch 60s each, Pigeon pose 60s each
EASY RUN + STRIDES: 55min Z2 + 6x20s strides. Strides should feel smooth and fast, not strained.

Details: 10min WU + 40min fartlek (vary effort by feel) + 10min CD
WorkoutDescription: FARTLEK: 'Speed play' - unstructured workout. After warm-up, vary effort by feel: surge for 1-3min (Z4), recover (Z2), sprint a short hill (Z5), jog easy, pick up pace on flat section, etc. 40min of playing with speed. Develops adaptability and race feel. Fun session!

Ultra Goal: Core anti-rotation endurance to prevent form collapse during the final hours of an ultra.

WARM-UP (5 min): Arm circles x15 each, Cat-cow x10, Thoracic rotations x10 each

MAIN BLOCK:
A1. Push-ups — 3x15 — Rest 60s
A2. Bent-over Rows — 3x12 — Rest 60s
B1. Shoulder Press — 3x10 — Rest 60s
B2. Single-leg Glute Bridge — 3x12 each — Rest 45s
C1. Single-leg Calf Raise — 3x15 each — Rest 45s

CORE CIRCUIT (x3, 45s rest between rounds):
Side Plank 40s each | Pallof Press 12 each | Russian Twists 15 each

MOBILITY (10 min): Hip flexor stretch 90s each, 90/90 stretch 60s each, Ankle dorsiflexion 60s each, Hamstring stretch 60s each

Details: Optional light jog or full rest
WorkoutDescription: REST: Rest before peak long run week.
LONG RUN WITH VERTICAL: 3h15 with 1200m+ D+. Practice power hiking technique on all steep uphills. Walk poles recommended. Fuel consistently. This simulates race conditions.
RECOVERY: Easy movement to flush legs.

Ultra Goal: Absorb 4 weeks of progressive loading — recover while maintaining neuromuscular patterns.

WARM-UP (5 min): Leg swings x10 each, Hip circles x10 each, Bodyweight squats x10

MAIN BLOCK (light):
A1. Bodyweight Bulgarian Split Squat — 2x8 each leg — Rest 45s
A2. Romanian Deadlift (50% weight) — 2x8 — Rest 60s
B1. Bodyweight Step-ups — 2x8 each leg — Rest 45s
B2. Glute Bridge — 2x12 — Rest 45s
C1. Calf Raise (bodyweight) — 2x15 — Rest 30s

CORE (1 round):
Plank 45s | Dead Bugs 10 reps | Side Plank 30s each

MOBILITY (15 min): Hip flexor stretch 90s each, Pigeon pose 90s each, 90/90 stretch 60s each, Ankle dorsiflexion 60s each, Thoracic foam roll 2 min, Hamstring stretch 60s each
DELOAD RUN: Easy 45min. Body absorbing previous training.
EASY WITH STRIDES: 40min easy + 4x15s strides to keep legs responsive. No hard efforts this week.
FULL REST: Complete recovery day.
REST OR EASY: Light movement only.
DELOAD LONG RUN: 2h easy. Flat preferred. No fueling stress - just easy running.
RECOVERY: Easy bike or walk.

Details: Split squat / RDL / step-downs / Nordic curls / plyos
WorkoutDescription:
Ultra Goal: Peak single-leg and eccentric strength — final hard block before race-specific taper begins.

WARM-UP (6 min): Leg swings x15 each, Hip circles x10 each, Glute bridges x15, A-skips x20

MAIN BLOCK:
A1. Bulgarian Split Squat — 4x8 each leg (heaviest yet) — Rest 75s
A2. Romanian Deadlift — 4x8 (heaviest yet) — Rest 90s
B1. Step-downs (3s eccentric) — 3x10 each leg — Rest 60s
B2. Hip Thrust — 3x12 — Rest 60s
C1. Box Jumps (power) — 3x6 — Rest 90s
C2. Single-leg Calf Raise — 3x15 each — Rest 45s
C3. Nordic Hamstring Curl — 3x8 — Rest 60s

CORE CIRCUIT (x3, 45s rest between rounds):
Plank 60s | Pallof Press 12 each | Dead Bugs 15 reps

COOL-DOWN (5 min): Hip flexor stretch 60s each, Pigeon pose 60s each
EASY RUN + STRIDES: 55min Z2 + 6x20s strides (Z5). Should feel good coming off deload.

THRESHOLD INTERVALS: 5x6min at threshold pace (HR 155-170). Shorter intervals but higher intensity than tempo. Should feel hard but controlled. 2min jog recovery between.

DOWNHILL REPEATS: After the main set, find a moderate gradient hill. 6x2min controlled fast downhill (same hill, reverse direction). Focus on short quick steps, slight forward lean, relax quads. Walk back up as recovery. This is CRITICAL for Balcons descents — eccentric quad strength is what separates finishers from sufferers in the final 20km.

Ultra Goal: Maintain upper body and core endurance; focus on recovery mobility before race-specific phase.

WARM-UP (5 min): Arm circles x15 each, Cat-cow x10, Thoracic rotations x10 each

MAIN BLOCK:
A1. Push-ups — 3x15 — Rest 60s
A2. Bent-over Rows — 3x12 — Rest 60s
B1. Shoulder Press — 3x10 — Rest 60s
B2. Single-leg Glute Bridge — 3x12 each — Rest 45s
C1. Single-leg Calf Raise — 3x15 each — Rest 45s

CORE CIRCUIT (x3, 45s rest between rounds):
Side Plank 45s each | Pallof Press 12 each | Russian Twists 15 each

MOBILITY (10 min): Hip flexor stretch 90s each, 90/90 stretch 60s each, Ankle dorsiflexion 60s each, Thoracic extension 2 min
FULL REST: Save energy for Saturday's big effort.

Details: Big effort - practice race day nutrition
WorkoutDescription: PEAK LONG RUN: 3h30 - biggest run of base phase. Practice EVERYTHING: race nutrition (60-80g carbs/hr), hydration, pacing, gear. This is a dress rehearsal. Stay in Z2 but expect fatigue in final hour.

BACK-TO-BACK: Running easy on fatigued legs from yesterday's long run. Keep HR Z1-Z2 strictly. 1h30 on trails at conversational pace. This teaches your body to run efficiently when glycogen-depleted — the key skill for 100km+ racing. Take in 30-40g carbs/h to practice fueling on tired legs.

Ultra Goal: Maintain peak strength without adding fatigue — bridge session into race-specific block.

WARM-UP (6 min): Leg swings x15 each, Hip circles x10 each, Glute bridges x15, Bodyweight squats x10

MAIN BLOCK:
A1. Bulgarian Split Squat — 3x8 each leg (same weight as W9) — Rest 75s
A2. Romanian Deadlift — 3x8 (same weight as W9) — Rest 90s
B1. Step-downs (2s eccentric) — 3x8 each leg — Rest 60s
B2. Hip Thrust — 3x10 — Rest 60s
C1. Single-leg Calf Raise — 3x12 each — Rest 45s
C2. Nordic Hamstring Curl — 3x6 — Rest 60s

CORE CIRCUIT (x3, 45s rest between rounds):
Plank 60s | Dead Bugs 15 reps | Side Plank 40s each

COOL-DOWN (5 min): Hip flexor stretch 60s each, Pigeon pose 60s each
EASY RUN: Standard Z2 aerobic run.
EASY + STRIDES: Light session. 45min easy + 4x20s strides to stay sharp.

Ultra Goal: Reduce training stress while keeping muscles engaged — transition to race-specific preparation.

WARM-UP (5 min): Leg swings x10 each, Hip circles x10 each, Bodyweight squats x10

MAIN BLOCK (reduced):
A1. Single-leg Deadlift (light) — 2x10 each — Rest 45s
A2. Single-leg Glute Bridge — 2x12 each — Rest 45s
B1. Single-leg Calf Raise — 2x15 each — Rest 30s

CORE CIRCUIT (x2, 45s rest):
Plank 50s | Dead Bugs 12 reps | Pallof Press 10 each

MOBILITY (15 min): Hip flexor stretch 90s each, Pigeon pose 90s each, 90/90 stretch 60s each, Ankle dorsiflexion 60s each, Thoracic foam roll 2 min, Hamstring stretch 60s each
FULL REST: Prepare for race simulation.

Details: Race simulation - full kit / nutrition / pacing
WorkoutDescription: EXTENDED RACE SIMULATION: 4h30-5h with race kit, poles (if using), and full nutrition protocol. This is your confidence builder before Balcons. Treat the first 3h as easy, final 1h30 at race effort. Practice aid station routine: what you grab, how long you stop. Aim for 60g carbs/h. Wear exact race shoes and pack.
RECOVERY: End of Phase 1. Congratulations on completing base build! Easy recovery today.

Details: Eccentric squats / step-downs / Nordic curls / core
WorkoutDescription:
Ultra Goal: Build eccentric quad and hamstring strength specifically to protect knees on Balcons' long descents.

WARM-UP (6 min): Leg swings x15 each, Hip circles x10 each, Glute bridges x15, Bodyweight lunges x10

MAIN BLOCK — ECCENTRIC FOCUS:
A1. Bulgarian Split Squat — 4x8 each @4010 tempo (4s down, 1s up) — Rest 90s
A2. Romanian Deadlift — 3x8 @3010 tempo (3s down) — Rest 90s
B1. Step-downs (slow 4s lowering) — 3x8 each leg — Rest 60s
B2. Nordic Hamstring Curl — 3x6 (slow eccentric) — Rest 60s
C1. Walking Lunges — 3x8 each @3010 tempo (3s down) — Rest 60s
C2. Single-leg Calf Raise (3s eccentric) — 3x12 each — Rest 45s

CORE CIRCUIT (x3, 45s rest between rounds):
Plank 60s | Pallof Press 12 each | Dead Bugs 15 reps

COOL-DOWN (5 min): Hip flexor stretch 60s each, Pigeon pose 60s each
EASY + STRIDES: 45min Z2 on trails + 5x20s strides. Get comfortable on technical terrain.
RACE PACE WORK: 4x8min at expected race effort (Z3-Z4). This is harder than tempo - closer to what you'll sustain in the race. 3min jog between. Learn what race pace feels like.

Ultra Goal: Single-leg stability and balance to prevent ankle rolls and maintain form on technical Balcons terrain.

WARM-UP (5 min): Leg swings x10 each, Hip circles x10 each, Single-leg balance 30s each

MAIN BLOCK — STABILITY FOCUS:
A1. Single-leg Deadlift — 3x8 each leg — Rest 60s
A2. Single-leg Squat (to box) — 3x8 each leg — Rest 60s
B1. Step-downs (controlled) — 3x8 each leg — Rest 60s
B2. Single-leg Glute Bridge — 3x10 each — Rest 45s
C1. Single-leg Calf Raise — 3x12 each — Rest 45s
C2. Nordic Hamstring Curl — 3x5 — Rest 60s

CORE CIRCUIT (x3, 45s rest between rounds):
Side Plank 40s each | Pallof Press 12 each | Dead Bugs 15 reps

MOBILITY (8 min): Hip flexor stretch 60s each, Ankle dorsiflexion 60s each, 90/90 stretch 60s each, Pigeon pose 60s each
REST OR EASY: Rest preferred.
COURSE SIMULATION: 3h30 with 1500m+ D+. If possible, run similar terrain to Balcons d'Azur. Practice race nutrition and pacing strategy.

BACK-TO-BACK for Balcons d'Azur prep: 1h30 easy on fatigued legs from yesterday's long run. Keep HR Z1-Z2 strictly. Running on glycogen-depleted legs is the most race-specific thing you can do for a 68k. Practice your race nutrition strategy.

Details: Maintenance: same load as W11 / eccentric / core
WorkoutDescription:
Ultra Goal: Maintain eccentric strength gains without adding new stimulus — hold steady approaching race day.

WARM-UP (6 min): Leg swings x15 each, Hip circles x10 each, Glute bridges x15, A-skips x20

MAIN BLOCK — MAINTAIN (same weight as W11):
A1. Bulgarian Split Squat — 3x8 each @4010 tempo — Rest 90s
A2. Romanian Deadlift — 3x8 @3010 tempo — Rest 90s
B1. Step-downs (4s lowering) — 3x8 each leg — Rest 60s
B2. Nordic Hamstring Curl — 3x6 — Rest 60s
C1. Walking Lunges — 3x8 each @3010 tempo — Rest 60s
C2. Single-leg Calf Raise (3s eccentric) — 3x12 each — Rest 45s

CORE CIRCUIT (x3, 45s rest between rounds):
Plank 60s | Dead Bugs 15 reps | Pallof Press 12 each

COOL-DOWN (5 min): Hip flexor stretch 60s each, Pigeon pose 60s each, Hamstring stretch 60s each
EASY RUN: Standard Z2 on trails.
HILL REPEATS: 6x3min hard uphill. Push into Z5 on final minute of each rep. This is last hard session before taper.
EASY RUN: Short and easy. Recovery from Wednesday.
FULL REST: Beginning pre-race rest.

Details: Last big long run before race
WorkoutDescription: FINAL LONG RUN: Last 3h run before Balcons. Keep it easy, don't push. Practice final gear check.
RECOVERY: Easy bike or walk. Taper begins.

Ultra Goal: Stay sharp without creating any muscle damage — race readiness is the priority.

WARM-UP (5 min): Leg swings x10 each, Hip circles x10 each, Bodyweight squats x10

MAIN BLOCK (50% weight, 2 sets only):
A1. Bulgarian Split Squat — 2x6 each (50% weight) — Rest 45s
A2. Romanian Deadlift — 2x6 (50% weight) — Rest 60s
B1. Step-ups (bodyweight) — 2x6 each — Rest 45s
B2. Glute Bridge — 2x10 — Rest 30s
C1. Calf Raise (bodyweight) — 2x12 — Rest 30s

CORE (1 round):
Plank 40s | Dead Bugs 8 reps | Side Plank 25s each

MOBILITY (15 min): Hip flexor stretch 90s each, Pigeon pose 90s each, 90/90 stretch 60s each, Hamstring stretch 60s each, Ankle dorsiflexion 60s each, Thoracic foam roll 2 min
TAPER RUN: Easy 40min. May feel heavy - that's normal during taper.

Details: Keep legs fresh with short accelerations
WorkoutDescription: SHAKEOUT: 30min easy + 4x30s strides (Z5). Strides keep neuromuscular system primed without creating fatigue.
FULL REST: Rest and prepare mentally.
SHORT EASY: 30min easy jog. Legs should start feeling fresh.
SHAKEOUT: 20min easy + 3x20s strides. Keep legs activated.
FULL REST: 6 days to race. Rest, hydrate, carb-load begins.
RACE WEEK SHAKEOUT: 25min very easy. Stay loose.
FULL REST: Focus on sleep and nutrition.
ACTIVATION: 15min easy + 3x20s strides (Z5). Brief efforts keep you sharp.
FULL REST: Two days to race.
REST OR WALK: 15-20min walk if restless, otherwise full rest.
PRE-RACE REST: Prepare gear, lay out nutrition, sleep early.

Details: First ultra test - conservative start - execute nutrition plan
WorkoutDescription: RACE DAY: Balcons d'Azur 68k. Start conservative! First 20km should feel easy. Execute nutrition plan (60-80g carbs/hr from start). Walk all steep uphills. This is a training race - finish strong, learn for Gypaète.
POST-RACE REST: Full rest. Eat well, hydrate, celebrate!

Details: Light walk only if feeling good
WorkoutDescription: ACTIVE RECOVERY: 20-30min walk if legs feel ok. No running.
TEST RUN: 30min very easy. Check how legs respond. Stop if any sharp pains.
FULL REST: Continue recovery.
EASY RUN: If Wednesday felt ok, 35min easy today.
EASY RUN: Building back. Still recovery week - don't push.
RECOVERY: Bike or walk. Prepare for Gypaète build next week.

Details: Light return: single-leg / glute bridge / core
WorkoutDescription:
Ultra Goal: Gentle return to strength work post-race — rebuild neuromuscular connection without overloading recovering tissues.

WARM-UP (5 min): Leg swings x10 each, Hip circles x10 each, Bodyweight squats x10

MAIN BLOCK (light — ease back in):
A1. Bodyweight Bulgarian Split Squat — 2x8 each leg — Rest 45s
A2. Romanian Deadlift (light) — 2x10 — Rest 60s
B1. Bodyweight Walking Lunges — 2x8 each leg — Rest 45s
B2. Glute Bridge — 2x12 — Rest 45s
C1. Single-leg Calf Raise — 2x12 each — Rest 30s

CORE CIRCUIT (x2, 45s rest):
Plank 40s | Dead Bugs 10 reps | Side Plank 25s each

MOBILITY (10 min): Hip flexor stretch 90s each, Pigeon pose 90s each, 90/90 stretch 60s each, Ankle dorsiflexion 60s each
EASY RUN: 50min Z2 on trails. Getting back to normal training.
TEMPO: Return to intensity. 2x12min tempo. Should feel manageable.

Ultra Goal: Rebuild core and upper body baseline while focusing on post-race mobility restoration.

WARM-UP (5 min): Arm circles x15 each, Cat-cow x10, Thoracic rotations x10 each

MAIN BLOCK:
A1. Push-ups — 2x8 — Rest 45s
A2. Bent-over Rows (light) — 2x10 — Rest 45s
B1. Single-leg Glute Bridge — 2x10 each — Rest 45s
B2. Single-leg Calf Raise — 2x12 each — Rest 30s

CORE CIRCUIT (x2, 45s rest):
Plank 40s | Pallof Press 8 each | Dead Bugs 10 reps

MOBILITY (12 min): Hip flexor stretch 90s each, Pigeon pose 90s each, 90/90 stretch 60s each, Thoracic foam roll 2 min, Ankle dorsiflexion 60s each, Hamstring stretch 60s each
REST OR EASY: Rest preferred.
LONG RUN: 2h easy. First real long run post-race. Don't push - just time on feet.
RECOVERY: Easy session.

Ultra Goal: Return to full strength sessions — rebuild single-leg power for Gypaète's mountain terrain.

WARM-UP (6 min): Leg swings x15 each, Hip circles x10 each, Glute bridges x15, A-skips x20

MAIN BLOCK:
A1. Bulgarian Split Squat — 3x8 each leg — Rest 75s
A2. Romanian Deadlift — 3x8 — Rest 90s
B1. Single-leg Step-ups — 3x8 each leg — Rest 60s
B2. Hip Thrust — 3x10 — Rest 60s
C1. Single-leg Calf Raise (full ROM) — 3x12 each — Rest 45s
C2. Nordic Hamstring Curl — 3x5 — Rest 60s

CORE CIRCUIT (x3, 45s rest between rounds):
Plank 50s | Dead Bugs 12 reps | Pallof Press 10 each

COOL-DOWN (5 min): Hip flexor stretch 60s each, Pigeon pose 60s each
EASY + STRIDES: 50min Z2 on hilly trails + 5x20s strides.

HILL REPEATS: 8x2min hard uphill. Push into Z5 territory. Mountain race prep.

DOWNHILL REPEATS: 6x2min controlled fast downhill on a moderate gradient. Focus on short quick steps, slight forward lean, relaxed quads. Walk back up as recovery. Critical for mountain racing — your quads need to handle thousands of meters of descent without blowing up.

Ultra Goal: Rebuild single-leg stability for technical mountain trail confidence after race recovery.

WARM-UP (5 min): Leg swings x10 each, Hip circles x10 each, Single-leg balance 30s each

MAIN BLOCK — STABILITY FOCUS:
A1. Single-leg Deadlift — 3x8 each — Rest 60s
A2. Single-leg Squat (to box) — 3x8 each — Rest 60s
B1. Step-downs (controlled) — 3x8 each leg — Rest 60s
B2. Single-leg Glute Bridge — 3x10 each — Rest 45s
C1. Single-leg Calf Raise — 3x12 each — Rest 45s

CORE CIRCUIT (x3, 45s rest between rounds):
Side Plank 35s each | Pallof Press 10 each | Dead Bugs 12 reps

MOBILITY (8 min): Hip flexor stretch 60s each, Ankle dorsiflexion 60s each, 90/90 stretch 60s each
REST: Rest before long run.
LONG RUN WITH VERTICAL: 2h30 with 1000m+ D+. Practice race hiking technique.
RECOVERY: Easy session post-vertical.

Details: Eccentric split squat / step-downs / Nordic curls / core
WorkoutDescription:
Ultra Goal: Eccentric strength for Gypaète's steep descents — slow negatives to build downhill resilience.

WARM-UP (6 min): Leg swings x15 each, Hip circles x10 each, Glute bridges x15, Bodyweight lunges x10

MAIN BLOCK — ECCENTRIC FOCUS:
A1. Bulgarian Split Squat — 4x8 each @4010 tempo (4s down) — Rest 90s
A2. Romanian Deadlift — 3x8 @3010 tempo — Rest 90s
B1. Step-downs (4s lowering) — 3x8 each leg — Rest 60s
B2. Nordic Hamstring Curl (slow eccentric) — 3x6 — Rest 60s
C1. Walking Lunges — 3x8 each @3010 tempo — Rest 60s
C2. Single-leg Calf Raise (3s eccentric) — 3x12 each — Rest 45s

CORE CIRCUIT (x3, 45s rest between rounds):
Plank 60s | Pallof Press 12 each | Dead Bugs 15 reps

COOL-DOWN (5 min): Hip flexor stretch 60s each, Pigeon pose 60s each
EASY RUN: 55min Z2 on trails.
TEMPO: 3x12min at threshold. Building race-specific endurance.

Ultra Goal: Recovery session — restore mobility after eccentric work, maintain upper body and core endurance.

WARM-UP (5 min): Arm circles x15 each, Cat-cow x10, Thoracic rotations x10 each

MAIN BLOCK:
A1. Push-ups — 3x12 — Rest 60s
A2. Bent-over Rows — 3x12 — Rest 60s
B1. Shoulder Press — 3x10 — Rest 60s
B2. Single-leg Glute Bridge — 3x10 each — Rest 45s
C1. Single-leg Calf Raise — 3x12 each — Rest 45s

CORE CIRCUIT (x3, 45s rest between rounds):
Russian Twists 15 each | Side Plank 40s each | Dead Bugs 15 reps

MOBILITY (10 min): Hip flexor stretch 90s each, 90/90 stretch 60s each, Ankle dorsiflexion 60s each, Thoracic extension 2 min
REST: Save energy for Saturday.
LONG RUN WITH VERTICAL: 3h with 1500m+ D+. Gypaète simulation. Practice everything.

BACK-TO-BACK for Gypaète prep: 1h30 easy on fatigued legs from yesterday's long run. Keep HR Z1-Z2 strictly. This simulates the second half of Gypaète when your legs are heavy. Practice eating real food (rice balls, potatoes) not just gels.

Ultra Goal: Maintain eccentric and single-leg strength at current level — consistency over progression this close to race.

WARM-UP (6 min): Leg swings x15 each, Hip circles x10 each, Glute bridges x15, A-skips x20

MAIN BLOCK:
A1. Bulgarian Split Squat — 3x8 each (same weight as W18) — Rest 75s
A2. Romanian Deadlift — 3x8 (same weight) — Rest 90s
B1. Step-downs (3s lowering) — 3x8 each leg — Rest 60s
B2. Hip Thrust — 3x10 — Rest 60s
C1. Single-leg Calf Raise — 3x12 each — Rest 45s
C2. Nordic Hamstring Curl — 3x6 — Rest 60s

CORE CIRCUIT (x3, 45s rest between rounds):
Plank 60s | Pallof Press 12 each | Side Plank 40s each

COOL-DOWN (5 min): Hip flexor stretch 60s each, Pigeon pose 60s each
EASY + STRIDES: 50min Z2 + 6x20s strides (Z5).
LONG HILL INTERVALS: 4x6min sustained hard uphill effort. Builds climbing endurance. Push to Z5 in final 2min of each.

Ultra Goal: Fine-tune single-leg balance and ankle stability for Gypaète's technical mountain sections.

WARM-UP (5 min): Leg swings x10 each, Hip circles x10 each, Single-leg balance 30s each

MAIN BLOCK — STABILITY FOCUS:
A1. Single-leg Deadlift — 3x8 each — Rest 60s
A2. Single-leg Squat (to box) — 3x8 each — Rest 60s
B1. Step-downs (controlled) — 3x8 each — Rest 60s
B2. Single-leg Glute Bridge — 3x10 each — Rest 45s
C1. Single-leg Calf Raise — 3x12 each — Rest 45s

CORE CIRCUIT (x3, 45s rest between rounds):
Pallof Press 12 each | Side Plank 40s each | Dead Bugs 15 reps

MOBILITY (8 min): Hip flexor stretch 60s each, Ankle dorsiflexion 60s each, 90/90 stretch 60s each, Pigeon pose 60s each
REST: Big weekend ahead.

GYPAÈTE-SPECIFIC LONG RUN: 4h with significant vertical gain. Find the biggest climb available and do a big loop: sustained 1h+ climb followed by technical descent. Practice race nutrition (60-80g carbs/h), poles on climbs, and power hiking technique. This is your peak long run before Gypaète — after this, trust the taper.
RECOVERY: Very important after peak effort.

Ultra Goal: Begin taper — reduce volume by 50% while maintaining neuromuscular readiness for race day.

WARM-UP (5 min): Leg swings x10 each, Hip circles x10 each, Bodyweight squats x10

MAIN BLOCK (50% weight, 2 sets):
A1. Bulgarian Split Squat — 2x6 each (50% weight) — Rest 45s
A2. Romanian Deadlift — 2x6 (50% weight) — Rest 60s
B1. Step-ups (bodyweight) — 2x6 each — Rest 45s
B2. Glute Bridge — 2x10 — Rest 30s
C1. Calf Raise (bodyweight) — 2x12 — Rest 30s

CORE (1 round):
Plank 40s | Dead Bugs 8 reps | Side Plank 25s each

MOBILITY (15 min): Hip flexor stretch 90s each, Pigeon pose 90s each, 90/90 stretch 60s each, Hamstring stretch 60s each, Ankle dorsiflexion 60s each, Thoracic foam roll 2 min
TAPER RUN: Easy 45min.
SHARPENING: 5x90s hill repeats (Z4) + 3x10s maximal hill sprints (Z5+). Keeps you sharp without fatigue.
FULL REST: Let body recover.
EASY: Short easy jog.

Details: Last long run - include some hills
WorkoutDescription: FINAL LONG RUN: 2h easy with some hills. Last real training before race.
RECOVERY: Easy bike or walk.

Details: Taper: very light movement / mobility only
WorkoutDescription:
Ultra Goal: Race week sharpening — minimal load to stay activated without any residual fatigue.

WARM-UP (5 min): Leg swings x10 each, Hip circles x10 each, Bodyweight squats x8

MAIN BLOCK (very light — activation only):
A1. Bodyweight Bulgarian Split Squat — 1x6 each — Rest 30s
A2. Bodyweight Walking Lunges — 1x6 each — Rest 30s
B1. Glute Bridge — 1x10 — Rest 30s
B2. Calf Raise (bodyweight) — 1x12 — Rest 30s

CORE (1 round):
Plank 30s | Dead Bugs 6 reps

MOBILITY (15 min): Hip flexor stretch 90s each, Pigeon pose 90s each, 90/90 stretch 90s each, Hamstring stretch 60s each, Ankle dorsiflexion 60s each, Thoracic foam roll 2 min, Quad foam roll 2 min
EASY: 35min easy.
SHAKEOUT: 25min easy + 4x30s strides (Z5).
FULL REST.
EASY: Very short easy jog.
SHAKEOUT: 15min easy + 3x20s strides.
FULL REST: Race week begins tomorrow.
RACE WEEK SHAKEOUT: 20min very easy.
FULL REST.
ACTIVATION: 15min easy + 3x20s strides.
FULL REST: Two days to race.
REST OR WALK: Very light if needed.

Details: Key mountain race - manage effort on climbs - trust training
WorkoutDescription: RACE DAY: Gypaète 76k. KEY MOUNTAIN RACE. Manage effort on climbs - walk steep sections. Execute nutrition. Trust your training from Balcons. This is your mountain test before Nice.
POST-RACE REST: Full rest. Recover well.
POST-RACE RECOVERY: Full rest day.
ACTIVE RECOVERY: Walk only if feeling ok.
TEST RUN: 30min very easy if legs feel recovered.
FULL REST.
EASY: Run or bike, whatever feels better.
EASY RUN: Building back gently.
RECOVERY: Easy bike spin.

Ultra Goal: Maintain baseline movement patterns during life transition — keep strength foundation alive with minimal time commitment.

WARM-UP (5 min): Leg swings x10 each, Hip circles x10 each, Bodyweight squats x10

MAIN BLOCK (bodyweight or very light):
A1. Bodyweight Bulgarian Split Squat — 2x8 each — Rest 45s
A2. Bodyweight Walking Lunges — 2x8 each — Rest 45s
B1. Glute Bridge — 2x12 — Rest 30s
B2. Single-leg Calf Raise — 2x12 each — Rest 30s

CORE (x2, 30s rest):
Plank 40s | Dead Bugs 10 reps

COOL-DOWN (5 min): Hip flexor stretch 60s each, Pigeon pose 60s each
EASY RUN: Stay active.
FLEXIBLE: Run or bike based on how you feel.
REST.
EASY: Standard easy run.
LONG RUN: 1h30 easy. Maintain endurance base.
RECOVERY: Easy movement.

Ultra Goal: Keep single-leg strength patterns alive — these are hard to rebuild from zero and critical for trail running.

WARM-UP (5 min): Leg swings x10 each, Hip circles x10 each, Bodyweight squats x10

MAIN BLOCK (light):
A1. Bodyweight Bulgarian Split Squat — 2x8 each — Rest 45s
A2. Romanian Deadlift (light) — 2x8 — Rest 60s
B1. Single-leg Glute Bridge — 2x10 each — Rest 30s
B2. Single-leg Calf Raise — 2x12 each — Rest 30s

CORE (x2, 30s rest):
Plank 45s | Side Plank 25s each | Dead Bugs 10 reps

COOL-DOWN (5 min): Hip flexor stretch 60s each, Pigeon pose 60s each
EASY RUN: Standard easy run.
SHORT TEMPO: 15min at threshold. Maintain some intensity.
REST.
FLEXIBLE: Easy movement.
LONG RUN: 1h30-2h if schedule allows.
RECOVERY: Easy.

Ultra Goal: Routine maintenance — keep gym habit alive even with baby arriving; any session is better than none.

WARM-UP (5 min): Leg swings x10 each, Hip circles x10 each, Bodyweight squats x10

MAIN BLOCK (light):
A1. Bodyweight Bulgarian Split Squat — 2x8 each — Rest 45s
A2. Bodyweight Walking Lunges — 2x8 each — Rest 45s
B1. Glute Bridge — 2x12 — Rest 30s
B2. Single-leg Calf Raise — 2x12 each — Rest 30s
C1. Nordic Hamstring Curl (eccentric only) — 2x4 — Rest 45s

CORE (x2, 30s rest):
Plank 40s | Pallof Press 8 each | Dead Bugs 10 reps

COOL-DOWN (5 min): Hip flexor stretch 60s each, Pigeon pose 60s each
EASY: Any movement counts.
EASY: Stay flexible with schedule.
REST.
FLEXIBLE: Whatever works.
FLEXIBLE LONG: Do what you can.
REST.

Details: Adapt to baby schedule - any movement counts
WorkoutDescription: SURVIVAL MODE: Baby arriving. Any exercise is a win. Run, bike, treadmill - whatever fits. Don't stress about missing workouts.

SURVIVAL MODE: Treadmill is your friend for quick sessions.

MINIMUM SESSION: Even if only 30min, include 10min at tempo effort. Maintaining threshold fitness through small consistent doses is more effective than occasional longer runs. Example: 10min easy warmup, 10min tempo (comfortably hard), 10min easy cooldown. This keeps your engine tuned even in survival mode.
SURVIVAL MODE: Stay consistent when possible.
SURVIVAL MODE: Rest if exhausted. Sleep > training right now.
SURVIVAL MODE: Move when you can.
SURVIVAL MODE: Try to protect one longer session if partner can cover.
REST: Family time.

Details: Baby arrival period - survive and stay active
WorkoutDescription: SURVIVAL MODE: First weeks with newborn. Any movement counts. Don't guilt yourself for missed sessions.

SURVIVAL MODE: Quick treadmill sessions work great.

MINIMUM SESSION: Even if only 30min, include 10min at tempo effort. Maintaining threshold fitness through small consistent doses is more effective than occasional longer runs. Your baby is your priority — but 30min of purposeful running keeps the Nice 110K dream alive.
SURVIVAL MODE: Consistency over intensity.
SURVIVAL MODE: Sleep if you need it.
SURVIVAL MODE: Move when possible.

Details: Protect one longer session if possible
WorkoutDescription: SURVIVAL MODE: Try for one longer session.
REST: Recover.

ADAPTATION: Finding routine. Move when you can.

MINIMUM SESSION: Even if only 30min, include 10min at tempo effort. Maintaining threshold fitness through small consistent doses is more effective than occasional longer runs. You're transitioning back to structure — every session counts.

Pre-build easy run: 45min at easy effort. The goal this week is simply to run consistently again. No pressure on pace — just get out the door and move. Your body remembers fitness better than you think.
Focus: Pre-Build: Reestablishing consistency before Nice block

WorkoutDescription: ADAPTATION: Any exercise is good.
Pre-build easy run: 45min at easy effort. Keep it conversational. If you're feeling good, add 4x20s strides at the end to wake up the legs.
Focus: Pre-Build: Reestablishing consistency before Nice block

WorkoutDescription: ADAPTATION: Movement helps mental health too.

Pre-build long run: 2h easy on trails. This is your reintroduction to longer efforts after the baby/flexible period. Keep HR in Z1-Z2. Bring nutrition and practice your race fueling. The Nice block starts next week — this is the foundation.
Focus: Pre-Build: Reestablishing consistency before Nice block

WorkoutDescription: RECOVERY: Light movement or rest.
RETURN TO STRUCTURE: Hopefully routine forming. Starting to rebuild.
EASY: Stay consistent.

QUALITY RETURNS: 15min easy warmup, then 20min at tempo effort (comfortably hard, ~Z3-Z4), then 15min easy cooldown. This is your first real quality session in weeks. Don't chase pace — go by feel and HR. The tempo should feel controlled, not desperate.
REST.
EASY: Standard easy run.

BUILDING BACK: 2h30 long run on trails. This is a step up from last week's 2h. Keep it easy (Z1-Z2) but include some hills to reintroduce vertical. Bring nutrition. You're 8 weeks from Nice — the fitness is coming back faster than you think.
RECOVERY: Easy.

Details: Return to strength: split squat / RDL / core
WorkoutDescription:
Ultra Goal: Rebuild strength foundation after baby period — ease back into loading with familiar movement patterns.

WARM-UP (6 min): Leg swings x15 each, Hip circles x10 each, Glute bridges x15, Bodyweight squats x10

MAIN BLOCK (moderate — rebuild):
A1. Bulgarian Split Squat — 3x8 each leg (start conservative) — Rest 60s
A2. Romanian Deadlift — 3x8 — Rest 90s
B1. Walking Lunges — 2x8 each — Rest 60s
B2. Glute Bridge — 3x12 — Rest 45s
C1. Single-leg Calf Raise — 3x12 each — Rest 45s
C2. Nordic Hamstring Curl (eccentric) — 2x5 — Rest 60s

CORE CIRCUIT (x2, 45s rest):
Plank 45s | Dead Bugs 10 reps | Side Plank 30s each

COOL-DOWN (5 min): Hip flexor stretch 60s each, Pigeon pose 60s each
EASY + STRIDES: 40min Z2 + 4x20s strides (Z5). Get neuromuscular system firing again.
TEMPO: 20min at threshold. Rebuilding intensity.
REST.
EASY: Standard easy run.
LONG RUN: 2h. Important for Nice prep.
RECOVERY: Easy session.

Details: Split squat / RDL / step-ups / Nordic curls / core
WorkoutDescription:
Ultra Goal: Full return to structured strength — build single-leg power for Nice-Cannes trail demands.

WARM-UP (6 min): Leg swings x15 each, Hip circles x10 each, Glute bridges x15, A-skips x20

MAIN BLOCK:
A1. Bulgarian Split Squat — 3x10 each leg — Rest 75s
A2. Romanian Deadlift — 3x10 — Rest 90s
B1. Single-leg Step-ups — 3x10 each leg — Rest 60s
B2. Hip Thrust — 3x12 — Rest 60s
C1. Single-leg Calf Raise (full ROM) — 3x15 each — Rest 45s
C2. Nordic Hamstring Curl — 3x6 — Rest 60s

CORE CIRCUIT (x3, 45s rest between rounds):
Plank 55s | Dead Bugs 12 reps | Pallof Press 10 each

COOL-DOWN (5 min): Hip flexor stretch 60s each, Pigeon pose 60s each
EASY + STRIDES: 45min Z2 on trails + 5x20s strides.
HILL REPEATS: 8x2min hard uphill. Push to Z5. Rebuild climbing power.

Ultra Goal: Rebuild single-leg stability and balance for Nice-Cannes' coastal trail technical sections.

WARM-UP (5 min): Leg swings x10 each, Hip circles x10 each, Single-leg balance 30s each

MAIN BLOCK — STABILITY FOCUS:
A1. Single-leg Deadlift — 3x8 each — Rest 60s
A2. Single-leg Squat (to box) — 3x8 each — Rest 60s
B1. Step-downs (controlled) — 3x8 each leg — Rest 60s
B2. Single-leg Glute Bridge — 3x10 each — Rest 45s
C1. Single-leg Calf Raise — 3x12 each — Rest 45s

CORE CIRCUIT (x3, 45s rest between rounds):
Side Plank 35s each | Pallof Press 10 each | Dead Bugs 12 reps

MOBILITY (8 min): Hip flexor stretch 60s each, Ankle dorsiflexion 60s each, 90/90 stretch 60s each
REST OR EASY: Rest preferred.
LONG RUN: 2h30. Rebuilding distance for Nice.
RECOVERY: Easy session.

Details: Split squat / RDL / eccentric step-downs / core
WorkoutDescription:
Ultra Goal: Introduce eccentric tempo for Nice-Cannes descent preparation — progressive overload from W32.

WARM-UP (6 min): Leg swings x15 each, Hip circles x10 each, Glute bridges x15, A-skips x20

MAIN BLOCK:
A1. Bulgarian Split Squat — 4x8 each @3010 tempo (3s down) — Rest 75s
A2. Romanian Deadlift — 3x10 (+2.5kg from W32) — Rest 90s
B1. Step-downs (3s eccentric) — 3x10 each leg — Rest 60s
B2. Hip Thrust — 3x12 — Rest 60s
C1. Single-leg Calf Raise (slow eccentric) — 3x15 each — Rest 45s
C2. Nordic Hamstring Curl — 3x6 — Rest 60s

CORE CIRCUIT (x3, 45s rest between rounds):
Plank 60s | Pallof Press 12 each | Dead Bugs 15 reps

COOL-DOWN (5 min): Hip flexor stretch 60s each, Pigeon pose 60s each
EASY + STRIDES: 50min Z2 + 6x20s strides.

TEMPO: 3x12min at threshold. Building race-specific fitness.

DOWNHILL COMPONENT: After the tempo block, add 6x2min fast controlled downhill repeats. By now your form should be dialed — focus on efficiency and relaxation at speed. This is Nice 110K-specific: the course has punishing descents that will destroy unprepared quads.

NIGHT RUNNING SESSION: Run with headlamp in the dark. 50min easy + 3x20s strides. Goal: get comfortable running at night, practice headlamp beam management, and simulate Nice 110K racing in darkness. This is essential for sub-18h racing. Tips: use a chest-mounted light in addition to headlamp for depth perception, carry a backup light, and practice eating while moving in the dark.
REST: Save for Saturday.
LONG RUN WITH VERTICAL: 3h with 1000m+ D+. Nice course simulation.

CRITICAL BACK-TO-BACK for Nice 110K prep. Running 2h on legs fatigued from yesterday's 3h30 build run. This is the most race-specific training you can do. Keep HR Z1-Z2, eat every 30min, and focus on maintaining good form even when tired. This simulates km 70-90 of Nice.

Details: Maintain: split squat / RDL / step-downs / core
WorkoutDescription:
Ultra Goal: Maintain peak eccentric and single-leg strength — no new load, just consistency approaching taper.

WARM-UP (6 min): Leg swings x15 each, Hip circles x10 each, Glute bridges x15, A-skips x20

MAIN BLOCK:
A1. Bulgarian Split Squat — 3x8 each @3010 tempo (same weight as W33) — Rest 75s
A2. Romanian Deadlift — 3x8 (same weight) — Rest 90s
B1. Step-downs (3s eccentric) — 3x8 each leg — Rest 60s
B2. Hip Thrust — 3x10 — Rest 60s
C1. Single-leg Calf Raise — 3x12 each — Rest 45s
C2. Nordic Hamstring Curl — 3x6 — Rest 60s

CORE CIRCUIT (x3, 45s rest between rounds):
Plank 60s | Pallof Press 12 each | Side Plank 40s each

COOL-DOWN (5 min): Hip flexor stretch 60s each, Pigeon pose 60s each
EASY + STRIDES: Standard easy + strides session.
LONG HILLS + SPRINTS: 4x6min sustained uphill (Z4-Z5) + 4x10s maximal hill sprints (Z5+). Big climbing session.

Ultra Goal: Final stability session before taper phase — lock in balance and ankle confidence for race terrain.

WARM-UP (5 min): Leg swings x10 each, Hip circles x10 each, Single-leg balance 30s each

MAIN BLOCK — STABILITY FOCUS:
A1. Single-leg Deadlift — 3x8 each — Rest 60s
A2. Single-leg Squat (to box) — 3x8 each — Rest 60s
B1. Step-downs (controlled) — 3x8 each — Rest 60s
B2. Single-leg Glute Bridge — 3x10 each — Rest 45s
C1. Single-leg Calf Raise — 3x12 each — Rest 45s

CORE CIRCUIT (x3, 45s rest between rounds):
Pallof Press 12 each | Side Plank 40s each | Dead Bugs 15 reps

MOBILITY (8 min): Hip flexor stretch 60s each, Ankle dorsiflexion 60s each, 90/90 stretch 60s each, Pigeon pose 60s each
REST: Save for peak long run.

PENULTIMATE BIG EFFORT: 4h30 with significant vertical gain. Include a mix of runnable trails and steep climbs to simulate Nice 110K terrain. Practice your full race nutrition plan (60-80g carbs/h). Run the first 3h easy, then push to race effort for the final 1h30. This is your second-to-last big session — make it count but stay injury-free.

PEAK BACK-TO-BACK WEEKEND for Nice 110K. Running 2h easy on legs fatigued from yesterday's 4h30 long run. This is the hardest back-to-back of the plan. Stay disciplined with HR Z1-Z2. Practice your full Nice nutrition plan. Mental rehearsal: visualize the Nice course during this run.

Details: Last hard session: eccentric / single-leg / plyos / core
WorkoutDescription:
Ultra Goal: Final high-intensity strength session — last chance to load before taper; make it count.

WARM-UP (6 min): Leg swings x15 each, Hip circles x10 each, Glute bridges x15, A-skips x20

MAIN BLOCK:
A1. Bulgarian Split Squat — 4x8 each @4010 tempo — Rest 90s
A2. Romanian Deadlift — 4x8 — Rest 90s
B1. Step-downs (4s eccentric) — 3x8 each leg — Rest 60s
B2. Nordic Hamstring Curl — 3x8 — Rest 60s
C1. Box Jumps (power) — 3x6 — Rest 90s
C2. Single-leg Calf Raise (3s eccentric) — 3x15 each — Rest 45s

CORE CIRCUIT (x3, 45s rest between rounds):
Plank 60s | Pallof Press 12 each | Dead Bugs 15 reps

COOL-DOWN (5 min): Hip flexor stretch 60s each, Pigeon pose 60s each
EASY + STRIDES: 50min Z2 + 6x20s strides.
RACE PACE: 35min at Nice race effort. Learn what sub-18h pace feels like. This is ~6km/h average.

Details: Reduced load: light single-leg / core / mobility
WorkoutDescription:
Ultra Goal: Begin volume reduction — keep muscles engaged but start dropping fatigue for race week.

WARM-UP (5 min): Leg swings x10 each, Hip circles x10 each, Bodyweight squats x10

MAIN BLOCK (reduced):
A1. Single-leg Deadlift (light) — 2x8 each — Rest 45s
A2. Single-leg Glute Bridge — 2x10 each — Rest 45s
B1. Single-leg Calf Raise — 2x12 each — Rest 30s
B2. Nordic Hamstring Curl (eccentric only) — 2x4 — Rest 45s

CORE CIRCUIT (x2, 45s rest):
Plank 45s | Dead Bugs 10 reps | Side Plank 30s each

MOBILITY (12 min): Hip flexor stretch 90s each, Pigeon pose 90s each, 90/90 stretch 60s each, Ankle dorsiflexion 60s each, Thoracic foam roll 2 min
FULL REST: Save everything for Saturday.

Details: Last big effort - full race nutrition practice
WorkoutDescription: PEAK LONG RUN — FINAL RACE SIM: 5h with full Nice 110K simulation. Race kit, nutrition every 45min (aim for 60-80g carbs/h), poles. Include a night section (start at dusk or run final 1h with headlamp). Practice headlamp management, eating in the dark, navigating with reduced visibility. This is the most important training session of the entire plan. After this, trust the work is done.
RECOVERY: Very easy. Taper begins now.

Ultra Goal: Taper week — maintain movement patterns at 50% load to stay sharp without accumulating fatigue.

WARM-UP (5 min): Leg swings x10 each, Hip circles x10 each, Bodyweight squats x10

MAIN BLOCK (50% weight, 2 sets):
A1. Bulgarian Split Squat — 2x6 each (50% weight) — Rest 45s
A2. Romanian Deadlift — 2x6 (50% weight) — Rest 60s
B1. Step-ups (bodyweight) — 2x6 each — Rest 45s
B2. Glute Bridge — 2x10 — Rest 30s
C1. Calf Raise (bodyweight) — 2x12 — Rest 30s

CORE (1 round):
Plank 40s | Dead Bugs 8 reps | Side Plank 25s each

MOBILITY (15 min): Hip flexor stretch 90s each, Pigeon pose 90s each, 90/90 stretch 60s each, Hamstring stretch 60s each, Ankle dorsiflexion 60s each, Thoracic foam roll 2 min
TAPER RUN: Easy 45min. May feel sluggish - normal.
SHARPENING: 20min tempo + 4x20s strides (Z5). Keep systems primed.
FULL REST.
EASY: 40min easy.
FINAL 2H RUN: Last longer effort. Keep it easy.
RECOVERY: Very easy.

Details: Taper: very light activation / mobility only
WorkoutDescription:
Ultra Goal: Race week — minimal activation to keep neuromuscular pathways firing without any tissue stress.

WARM-UP (5 min): Leg swings x10 each, Hip circles x10 each, Bodyweight squats x8

MAIN BLOCK (activation only):
A1. Bodyweight Bulgarian Split Squat — 1x6 each — Rest 30s
A2. Bodyweight Walking Lunges — 1x6 each — Rest 30s
B1. Glute Bridge — 1x10 — Rest 30s
B2. Calf Raise (bodyweight) — 1x12 — Rest 30s

CORE (1 round):
Plank 30s | Dead Bugs 6 reps

MOBILITY (15 min): Hip flexor stretch 90s each, Pigeon pose 90s each, 90/90 stretch 90s each, Hamstring stretch 60s each, Ankle dorsiflexion 60s each, Thoracic foam roll 2 min, Quad foam roll 2 min
EASY: 35min easy.
SHAKEOUT: 25min easy + 4x30s strides (Z5).
FULL REST.
EASY: Short easy jog. Legs should feel fresh.
SHAKEOUT: 20min easy + 3x20s strides.
FULL REST: Race week begins.
RACE WEEK: 20min very easy shakeout.
FULL REST: Focus on sleep, nutrition, hydration.
ACTIVATION: 15min easy + 3x20s strides (Z5). Brief sharp efforts.
FULL REST: Carb loading. Hydrate well.
REST OR WALK: Light walk if restless.
PRE-RACE: Prepare all gear. Lay out nutrition. Sleep early. Trust your training.

Details: GOAL RACE - Execute plan - sub-18h - trust your training!
WorkoutDescription: GOAL RACE: Nice 110K. Sub-18h target (~6km/h avg). START CONSERVATIVE! First 30km should feel easy. Execute nutrition plan from kilometer 0 (60-80g carbs/hr). Walk all steep uphills. Stay calm through low points - they pass. You've done the work. TRUST YOUR TRAINING!
